title: FD293901: Solve root cause of restriction list data missing for random products for multiple customers
description:
I've confirmed repeated incorrect states of restriction list / substance data issues. Can we monitor how common this is and make changes to CPU to cover these scenarios so that our data self-heals? Use monitoring of the issues to see that changes we make has an impact.

Facts known:

It's common that data related to why a product is on list is missing in customer systems. (references to substances)

I've seen two variations of incorrect states for this, one where the product still have references to old version of substances in the product data, but restriction list data is updated and referring to the new substances.

In the other case data is missing but for correct substances.

There's a known issue for the case where the customer uses product application and picks a product from the global database, let's see that as a separate issue. This task is about the cases where the customer have the product active in inventory.

Scenario 1:

```sql
-- Here's a query to detect one of the cases. Would be good to monitor all customer systems for this issue:
select top 10000 prod.ArtNo, prod.ModifiedDate, mainprod.ModifiedDate, *
from GetInventory(0, 10) inv
join tblMainRegArticle prod
on inv.AttID = prod.ID and inv.AttID_mdbID = prod.ID_mdbID
left join MultiDB..localMainRegArticle mainprod
on prod.ID = mainprod.ID and prod.ID_mdbID = mainprod.ID_mdbID
where exists (
	select *
	from MultiDB..localDocLnkCAS multi
	where multi.AttID = prod.ID and multi.AttID_mdbID = prod.ID_mdbID
	and not exists (
		select *
		from tblDocLnkCAS localsubst
		where localsubst.AttID = multi.AttID and localsubst.AttID_mdbID = multi.AttID_mdbID
		and localsubst.CASAttID = multi.CASAttID and localsubst.CASAttID_mdbID = multi.CASAttID_mdbID
	)
)
and not mainprod.ModifiedDate > prod.ModifiedDate -- try to exclude products that recently have been updated by SDS team and not updated locally yet.
```

Scenario 2:

```
-- Here's a query to detect one of the cases. Would be good to monitor all customer systems for this issue:
select top 10000 prod.ArtNo, prod.ModifiedDate, mainprod.ModifiedDate, *
from GetInventory(0, 10) inv
join tblMainRegArticle prod
on inv.AttID = prod.ID and inv.AttID_mdbID = prod.ID_mdbID
left join MultiDB..localMainRegArticle mainprod
on prod.ID = mainprod.ID and prod.ID_mdbID = mainprod.ID_mdbID
where exists (
	select *
	from MultiDB..localMainLnkLists l
	where l.AttID = prod.ID and l.AttID_mdbID = prod.ID_mdbID
	and not exists (
		select *
		from tblMainLnkLists locall
		where locall.AttID = l.AttID and locall.AttID_mdbID = l.AttID_mdbID
		and locall.ListID = l.ListID and locall.ListID_mdbID = l.ListID_mdbID
	)
)
and not mainprod.ModifiedDate > prod.ModifiedDate -- try to exclude products that recently have been updated by SDS team and not updated locally yet.
```

If needed we can discuss strategies for monitor + making tweaks/fixes and follow impact on the fixes.

PS. It might be worth it to take a look at the timing of SDS Tools and CPU to make sure we don't have an issue if SDS team makes more than one change within a CPU cycle.

title: FD291724: View for showing all one time actions via overview returns empty page
description:
Reproduced by having at least one onetime action created in a risk assessment. Go to overview and find the one time action box. Click on View All.

The issue seems to be related to a view that the RiskAssessmentMainController returns from the action ShowAllMeasures. The view seem to not support the new front-end logic. The backend returns correct result as can be seen in the browser logs.

title: FD293685: Change UI limit of comment field for restriction list to what database support
description:
![image.png](https://uploads.linear.app/d040f0e9-edba-4253-a021-5028751c4dfb/93df3bf2-5038-47c0-8fcd-f422d437678b/e8831c2b-3734-49d9-b222-b1bbb1035d04)

I've confirmed in [SLS-264](https://linear.app/intersolia/issue/SLS-264/fd293685-investigate-data-length-limitation-for-restriction-list) that we have a UI limit of \~255 characters even though our database supports 4000 characters. This was not true before UI was upgraded with new look and capabilities by Serbian dev team. We have a customer who have existing longer comments that they can't work with now.

Can you make the limit correct? I think also we have similar issues in more places in the system where we seem to have a generic limit of \~255 characters. For example in the comment field for approving a inventory request comment field seem to be looked to \~255 chars event though UI indicates we support 512 characters. General CSS issue?

title: Button sets in risk assessment are not multi-click safe
description:
After dev team changed the UI of the button sets in risk assessment it is now possible to cause navigation history to be corrupted by clicking multiple times or clicking more than one button after each other. This leads to iChemistry seemingly randomly navigating you to for example the start page or somewhere else you have visisted.

We need to make the buttons safe from multiple clicks. We have multiple customers complaining that the risk assessment module "throws them out".

See [SLS-113](https://linear.app/intersolia/issue/SLS-113/fd288216-more-issues-with-navigation-history-in-risk-assessment) and [SLS-102](https://linear.app/intersolia/issue/SLS-102/fd287698-investigate-issue-with-risk-assessment-done-button)

Validate that uploaded files in Application Flex is still in session on submit
description:
See [SLS-50](https://linear.app/intersolia/issue/SLS-50/fd277356-investigate-broken-links-to-uploaded-files)

Today there's a scenario where file gets temporarily stored in session, and somehow lost, if file can't be fetched from session we store the temporary link to the session object instead of a valid URL.

Either we validate and warn the user on form submit, or less nice solution is to at least throw a real error to the user (meaning they'd have to redo the application).

In contrast to what has sometimes happened now, where we allow broken links that are persisted, that the user later expects to work.

title: Change password on seq logging service for Dataaccess
description:
Hosted at [http://4.207.208.162/](https://4.207.208.162/)

kubectl exec -n seq -it my-seq-748df69f7c-fx9xm -- /seqsvr/Seq auth reset --storage=/data --username=admin --password=test

It fails as it needs exclusive lock on the data folder.

In order to solve it we have to scale the pod to 0 instances, create a new temporary pod with the same data mounted and without the service running - run the command and then scale up again.

title: FD277260: Unable to download documents on certain devices (OpenAccess)
description:
Users are unable to open or download documents (e.g., safety sheets) on some mobile devices, primarily Android. The issue seems related to how the client currently handles file downloads.

At the moment, the client creates a download link programmatically using a base64-encoded document string and then simulates a click to trigger the download. This approach appears to fail or behave inconsistently on Android devices, where the file either doesn’t download at all or produces no visible result.

**What we know:**

* The issue occurs mainly on Android devices.
* iOS seems less affected.
* Several client-side error handling approaches have been tested, but the issue persists.
* The underlying cause is likely how base64 data URLs are handled on mobile browsers when used to simulate downloads.

**Next step / Proposed solution:**
Refactor the download flow so that documents are **served directly from the backend** instead of being returned as a base64-encoded string.

* Implement a proper download endpoint returning a binary stream with correct `Content-Type` and `Content-Disposition` headers.
* The client should receive a real URL to the file and trigger a standard download request.
* This should ensure consistent behavior across all devices and browsers.

title: FD277035: RA Signed tab can load data from wrong organisation node when coming from Overview
description:
If you use Overview and click on any of the Todo-numbers for risk assessment and then click on a sub-department that is different from the organisation node you selected in the tree, then risk assessment list, signed tab will use wrong organisation IDs, resulting in a different data set compared to doing the equivalent operation in risk assessment module.

See image for example:

![image.png](https://uploads.linear.app/d040f0e9-edba-4253-a021-5028751c4dfb/38bea43e-c4d3-47e2-ae6d-bc1cb815ade4/6cf7d646-89e1-449c-b461-1e095fc657c1)

This worked before dev team introduced new UI for riskassessment and/or dataaccess (that is currently turned off for RA Signed Tab.

Additionally this code from RiskAssessment_v3.vue, line 140 is suspected to be involved in the mistake:

```
    var node = (this.fromPage && this.orgId) ? node = { KeyID: this.orgId.split(':')[0], KeyID_mdbID : this.orgId.split(':')[1] } : window.SideTree.GetSelectedNode();
    axios.get(`/RiskAssessmentList/${this.signedRisksURL}?orgID=${node.KeyID}&orgID_mdbID=${node.KeyID_mdbID}`)
```
